summary: The session showed some signs of fatigue, particularly in the bicep exercises where the user hit a bottleneck earlier than planned. However, the user adapted well by adjusting exercise order and rep schemes to maintain intensity.

per_exercise.observation: The first set was cut short for setup adjustments, but the subsequent sets hit the target RPE and rep range. The dropset was completed as planned.
per_exercise.suggested_note_change:
Target: 3 Technical Failure (RPE 10).

Lengthened partials: Immediately after third set, log as drop set.

Setup: Cable #11 | Bench #7. If can, use chest support.

Elbow Lead: Hands are hooks; pull through the elbows.

Tear: Pull toward hairline; tear the rope apart".

Double Bicep: Finish with hands higher than elbows.

Vector: 45° upward angle (toward forehead).

Session Notes: Stopped set 1 early adjusting height for beter clearance to forehead.

Set 2 trying pulley height 7, seat about 45 degrees. Rope can come through top of seat.
Set 3 trting lower pulley height 6.

per_exercise.observation: The user hit a bicep bottleneck early in the second and third sets, significantly reducing reps. The planned 3 failure sets were extended to include a dropset to compensate for the reduced volume on the failure sets.
per_exercise.suggested_note_change:
Target: 3 Technical Failure (RPE 10).

Setup: Neutral/vertical grip. Palms facing.

Pockets: Pull shoulder blades toward back pockets before arm movement.

Sternum: Chest "glued" to pad.

Crush: Imagine crushing grape between shoulder blades at peak.

Hook: Hands are thumbless hooks. Focus elbow drive.

Mid-Range: Stop 2–3cm before full contraction. Avoid bicep takeover.

Session Notes: Matrix plate loaded seated row - assuming total base resistance is 11.3kg.

Set 1: bicep bottle neck at around 10 reps
Set 2: 2 min rest, bicep bottle neck at around 6 reps
Set 3: 2.5 min rest, bicep bottle neck at rep 5

per_exercise.observation: The user moved this exercise earlier due to unavailable equipment and encountered a bicep bottleneck in the first three sets, reducing reps significantly. They then performed an additional set to failure, pushing to a very high rep count, indicating a potential RPE misjudgment but still achieving high volume.
per_exercise.suggested_note_change:
Target: 3 Technical Failure (RPE 10).

Tempo: Slow controlled.

Window: Head "tall" looking through window. Pull to upper chest, not stomach.

Elbow Tuck: Drive elbows toward hips. Do not pull behind body.

Leg Anchor: Push thighs into pads. Create "closed loop" tension.

Session Notes: Doing this prior to assisted pull ups as none available.

using double v handle (aka prone grip) with smaller side of grip facing me

Set 1 on rep 5 bicep bottlenexk
Set 2 rested 2.5 mins instead of 2, bicep bottleneck rep 5
Set 3 rested 2.5 mins instead of 2, bicep bottleneck rep 5/6 
Set 4 limit testing, slow and controlled, at rep 14 i thought i was 6 RPE, but kept going (evidence of extremely skewed RPE gauge), bicep bottleneck at rep 32

per_exercise.observation: The user hit a bicep bottleneck after the second set and was unable to perform the planned dropset. The routine notes should be updated to reflect the consistent use of an EZ bar.
per_exercise.suggested_note_change:
Target: 2 Technical Failure (RPE 10).

Lengthened partials: Immediately after third set, log as drop set, bottom half of movement.

Armpit: Jam armpits deep into pad. No "air" between triceps and bench.

Wrist: Keep straight or curled inward. Avoid wrists "flopping" back.

Pinky: "Turn" pinkies toward shoulders during curl. Maximize supination.

Safety Stop: Stop short of 180° extension. Protect bicep tendon.

Shoulder: Keep shoulders depressed. Do not shrug toward ears.

Session Notes: always EZ bar as always at preacher curls, add to routine notes

rest default is 1.5m lets change to 2min

biceps gassed after set 2 at the bottom, couldnt even get into position for partials

per_exercise.observation: The user exceeded the rep target on the first set and completed all planned sets and the dropset, indicating good performance despite potential fatigue from previous exercises.
per_exercise.suggested_note_change:
Target: 2 Technical Failure (RPE 10).

Lengthened partials: Immediately after third set, log as drop set.

Setup: Sit tall; barely touch chest pad. Force core active.

Pinky Lead: Light grip; push out with pinky fingers. Bypass traps.

Wide: Reach for side walls with knuckles. Reach "out and wide", not "back".

Elbow Lock: Soft bend; lock angle. Prevent tricep takeover.

Stop Early: Stop when arms line with shoulders. Avoid shoulder "roll" forward.

Session Notes: What angle do my arms need to be to the floor? Parallel or slightly downafds/upwards

Set 1, had the seat higher than usual arms are parallel with floor, towards end of set can feel it in my mid right back

per_exercise.observation: The user struggled to maintain the hold duration from the previous session, indicating fatigue. They adjusted rest times and rep counts accordingly. The note about left side starting position should be added.
per_exercise.suggested_note_change:
Target: 3 Technical Failure (RPE 10).

Holds: Seated; 15 seconds per rep.

Setup: Sit sideways. Pull handle to chest center.

Anti-Slouch: "Punch" shoulder blades forward (protraction). Activate serratus.

Anchor: Brace feet against machine frame. Anchor legs for anti-rotation.

Friction: ~15kg load causes seat sliding. Stabilize with leg pressure.

Session Notes: Add to notes i always start with left side closer to cable, with handle on left hand

Set 1 held 4 reps 10 seconds , last workout was 3 reps 15 seconds - was struggling already at 10 likely torched 
set 2 rest 1 min default, 3 reps of 10 seconds
set 3 rest 2 min for data, 3 reps of 15 seconds,
